lede: A look at September 27 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jenna Ortega and Massimo Bertocchi, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War
events.desc: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: Azerbaijan launches an offensive against the self-proclaimed Republic of Artsakh in the Nagorno-Karabakh region, inhabited predominantly by ethnic Armenians.

events.title: Google
events.desc: The Google internet search engine retroactively claims this date as its birthday.

events.title: BAC TSR-2
events.desc: The British TSR-2 aircraft XR219 makes its maiden flight.

events.title: Yemen Arab Republic
events.desc: The Yemen Arab Republic is established.

events.title: SS Patrick Henry
events.desc: The SS Patrick Henry is launched, becoming the first of more than 2,700 Liberty ships.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Tripartite Pact is signed in Berlin by Germany, Japan and Italy.

events.title: Bobby Jones (golfer)
events.desc: Bobby Jones wins the (pre-Masters) Grand Slam of golf.

events.title: Lij Iyasu
events.desc: Iyasu V is proclaimed deposed as ruler of Ethiopia in a palace coup in favor of his aunt Zewditu.

events.title: Ellen Southard
events.desc: The merchant sailing ship Ellen Southard is wrecked in a storm at Liverpool.

events.title: History of the Jews in France
events.desc: The National Assembly of France votes to award full citizenship to Jews.

births.name: Matt Harding
births.line: American video game designer and dancer

births.name: Shaun Cassidy
births.line: American actor, singer, producer, and screenwriter

births.name: Paul Reichmann
births.line: Austrian-Canadian businessman, founded Olympia and York (died 2013)

deaths.text: Russell M. Nelson, 17th President of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints
Vincent de Paul, French priest and saint
